lines.line: > The speaker, a history major at the College of Idaho, introduces the purpose of the interview with Jan Ronald Boyles, a retired archivist and environmental photographer. The interview is part of a collaboration with the Idaho State Historical Society and is supported by the NEA. The goal is to reflect on Boyles' personal experiences and the environmental history of Treasure Valley, Idaho.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, a history major at the College of Idaho, introduces the purpose of the interview with Jan Ronald Boyles, a retired archivist and environmental photographer. The interview is part of a collaboration with the Idaho State Historical Society and is supported by the NEA. The goal is to reflect on Boyles' personal experiences and the environmental history of Treasure Valley, Idaho.

lines.line: # Early Life and World War II Impact

> The speaker was born in Fulton, Missouri, just before the United States entered World War II. His father's role as a professor and later as a Navy lieutenant during the war led the family to travel across the country. After the war, they settled in Dallas, Texas, where the speaker grew up and developed an interest in photography. A visit to his uncle in Idaho sparked his love for the state's natural beauty and led to his decision to move there for college.

metadata.title: Early Life and World War II Impact
metadata.synopsis: The speaker was born in Fulton, Missouri, just before the United States entered World War II. His father's role as a professor and later as a Navy lieutenant during the war led the family to travel across the country. After the war, they settled in Dallas, Texas, where the speaker grew up and developed an interest in photography. A visit to his uncle in Idaho sparked his love for the state's natural beauty and led to his decision to move there for college.

lines.line: > The speaker has lived in Caldwell, Idaho, since 1963 and has observed its transformation from a predominantly agricultural area to one with increasing suburban development. He expresses dismay at the traffic congestion and the changes in the landscape, noting the impact on local wildlife refuges and his own shift away from hunting and fishing to focus on photography.

metadata.title: Experiencing Caldwell's Transformation
metadata.synopsis: The speaker has lived in Caldwell, Idaho, since 1963 and has observed its transformation from a predominantly agricultural area to one with increasing suburban development. He expresses dismay at the traffic congestion and the changes in the landscape, noting the impact on local wildlife refuges and his own shift away from hunting and fishing to focus on photography.

lines.line: > The speaker discusses the importance of personal networking in his photography career and his early efforts to get his work published. He reflects on his involvement in environmental activism before the establishment of federal environmental protection agencies, highlighting grassroots efforts to preserve Idaho's natural resources and the role of former Governor Cecil Andrus in environmental governance.

metadata.title: Photography and Environmental Activism
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the importance of personal networking in his photography career and his early efforts to get his work published. He reflects on his involvement in environmental activism before the establishment of federal environmental protection agencies, highlighting grassroots efforts to preserve Idaho's natural resources and the role of former Governor Cecil Andrus in environmental governance.

lines.line: # Transition from Film to Digital Photography

> The speaker recounts his transition from working as a college photographer to a photojournalist at a local newspaper, where he produced a popular feature series called the Snake Basin Explorer. He also discusses the impact of the digital revolution on photography and archiving, noting the advantages and challenges it brought to his work.

metadata.title: Transition from Film to Digital Photography
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts his transition from working as a college photographer to a photojournalist at a local newspaper, where he produced a popular feature series called the Snake Basin Explorer. He also discusses the impact of the digital revolution on photography and archiving, noting the advantages and challenges it brought to his work.

lines.line: > The speaker feels a strong sense of community in the Treasure Valley, having made many friends and contributed to local history. However, he acknowledges feeling like an outsider because he did not graduate from Caldwell High School, a bond he observes as significant among locals. He also shares anecdotes about his uncle, a respected biology teacher.

metadata.title: Community Ties and Idahoan Identity
metadata.synopsis: The speaker feels a strong sense of community in the Treasure Valley, having made many friends and contributed to local history. However, he acknowledges feeling like an outsider because he did not graduate from Caldwell High School, a bond he observes as significant among locals. He also shares anecdotes about his uncle, a respected biology teacher.

lines.line: > The speaker compares his childhood experiences during the polio epidemic to the recent Covid-19 pandemic, lamenting the low vaccination rates in his county and the rejection of scientific medicine by many. He reflects on the importance of vaccines in combating diseases and expresses concern about the polarization and denial of science in contemporary society.

metadata.title: Comparing Polio and Covid-19 Pandemics
metadata.synopsis: The speaker compares his childhood experiences during the polio epidemic to the recent Covid-19 pandemic, lamenting the low vaccination rates in his county and the rejection of scientific medicine by many. He reflects on the importance of vaccines in combating diseases and expresses concern about the polarization and denial of science in contemporary society.

lines.line: > The speaker shares his observations of environmental degradation, including the decline in bird species and insects in his area. He expresses concern for the future of the Treasure Valley, noting the population growth and changes in cultural interests. He also discusses the impact of technology on the environment and the challenges of planning for the future amid uncertainty.

metadata.title: Environmental Degradation and the Future
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares his observations of environmental degradation, including the decline in bird species and insects in his area. He expresses concern for the future of the Treasure Valley, noting the population growth and changes in cultural interests. He also discusses the impact of technology on the environment and the challenges of planning for the future amid uncertainty.

lines.line: > The speaker reflects on the interview process, expressing appreciation for the questions asked and the opportunity to share his experiences. He acknowledges the potential for such interviews to be boring but hopes his responses have been engaging. The speaker also looks forward to the possibility of accessing other interviews from the project.

metadata.title: Reflections on the Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the interview process, expressing appreciation for the questions asked and the opportunity to share his experiences. He acknowledges the potential for such interviews to be boring but hopes his responses have been engaging. The speaker also looks forward to the possibility of accessing other interviews from the project.
